scope.question: How do different modeling assumptions for electroweak first-order phase transitions move the GW signal in the (f, ω_GW) plane?
in_scope: Consistent combination of bubble collisions, sound waves, and turbulence in GW spectra
Testing of wall velocity and sound shell model assumptions
Comparison with prior benchmarks (e.g., Hindmarsh et al. 2015)
out_of_scope: Gravitational wave background from other sources
unresolved_questions: Which specific sound shell model to prioritize?
Which turbulence decay rates to consider?

crucial_inputs: Wall velocity and sound shell model test cases

formulations: GW spectra from electroweak first-order phase transitions

observables.name: GW Spectra (f, ω_GW)
observables.definition: Gravitational wave energy density spectra as a function of frequency

claims.statement: Consistent combination of bubble collisions, sound waves, and turbulence in GW spectra moves the signal in the (f, ω_GW) plane.

deliverables.description: Code for generating improved GW spectra for electroweak phase transitions
must_contain: bubble collision, sound wave, and turbulence combinations

deliverables.description: Plots comparing the GW spectra under different modeling assumptions
New analytical formula for the improved GW spectrum
must_contain: derivation steps and comparison with existing benchmarks

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-gw-consistency
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare the generated GW spectra against Hindmarsh et al. (2015) and existing benchmarks
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Reasonable agreement with benchmarks in appropriate limits

references.locator: Hindmarsh et al., Phys. Rev. D 92, 123009 (2015)
references.why_it_matters: Primary benchmark for cosmological first-order phase transitions

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-gw-consistency
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Qualitative agreement without rigorous benchmark comparison
forbidden_proxies.reason: Modeling assumptions must be quantitatively tested against ground truth

disconfirming_observations: Signal moving unexpectedly outside (f, ω_GW) plane benchmarks

open_questions: Which specific sound shell model to prioritize?
Which turbulence decay rates to consider?

convention_lock.index_positioning: p^0 = omega (energy), Greek spacetime, Latin spatial
